broker_fee_detail: FARE Act notice shown; listed by AM Residential leasing team. 'Small pets OK.'
broker_message: Hi, I'm interested in 1320 Coney Island Ave #41. Can you confirm it's a 4th-floor walk-up, the square footage, and whether it's rent-stabilized (with rent history)? I saw it was in contract at $2,390 last December before the price came down, and city records show open fire-escape/egress and heat/hot-water items — how have those been addressed? Given nearby 1-beds around $1,850-$1,900, would the owner consider $1,900? I can tour this week.
broker_questions: Confirm it's a 4th-floor walk-up — how many flights?
Why did the December 2025 contract at $2,390 not close, and is $1,995 firm?
City record shows obstructed fire-escape/egress violations — resolved?
How has heat/hot water performed the last two winters?
What size pets are allowed, any pet deposit?
Total move-in costs and any tenant-paid fee?
building_risk_rationale: Augrented: 10 HPD violations with 17 shown open (below Midwood avg 34.8) and 37 lifetime 311 calls (near avg 36.7), so on raw counts around neighborhood-typical for a 32-unit walk-up. Raising to Medium: 87% 12-month failure forecast, recurring Heat/Hot-Water (Class C 'no heat' 3B; building/apartment complaints Jan-Feb 2026), repeated 'HPD unable to gain access', an emergency plumbing complaint (May 2026), and three ACTIVE ECB penalties ~$22,500 including $6,250 (May 2026) and $6,250/$10,000 (2023) for OBSTRUCTED FIRE-ESCAPE landings and unpermitted scaffolding — an egress-safety flag. Per-unit open load (17/32) elevated; bedbugs clean since 2019.
commute_notes: Closest is the Q (Brighton) at Avenue J, ~6 min — the listing's 'nearby Q train' is accurate. Q express to Midtown ~45-55 min, one of the better south-Brooklyn commutes. B runs rush-hours only.
concessions_to_ask: Written fire-escape/egress and heat repair assurances
cons: Top-floor walk-up in a 1926 building (no elevator)
Active obstructed-fire-escape/egress ECB violations plus recurring heat/hot-water complaints
Elevated open-violation load per unit (17/32); busy commercial-corridor noise
leverage_notes: Strong leverage: price already cut ~17% and a prior contract collapsed, 15 days out, top-floor walk-up (a real turnoff at this rent). Comps $1,800-$2,000. A push to $1,900 or a concession is well supported; active fire-escape violations add a bargaining point.
noise_risks: Coney Island Avenue is a busy commercial corridor — traffic, buses, storefront noise; top-floor unit mitigates street noise somewhat
Elevated Brighton line a few blocks east
plain_english: The most negotiable of the group. Price already fell ~17% (a prior $2,390 contract fell through), so at $1,995 it's fair, no fee, small pets OK, and a 6-minute walk to the Q. Catches: 4th-floor walk-up in a 1926 building, and active fire-escape/egress violations plus recurring heat complaints. Not stale at 15 days, but with a fresh cut and a collapsed prior deal you have real leverage — tour it, inspect stairs and fire escape, confirm stabilization, and try for $1,900 or a concession.
pros: Big ~17% price cut to a now-fair $1,995, no fee, small pets OK
6-min walk to the Q at Avenue J — a solid Midtown commute
1-bed with high ceilings, three closets; likely rent-stabilized

red_flags.how_to_verify: Confirm walk-up and count flights on tour; assess stairs
red_flags.issue: Top floor (4th) of a four-story WALK-UP
red_flags.why: No elevator; four flights every trip

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask why the Dec 2025 contract didn't close and whether the price is firm
red_flags.why: Unit was 'in contract' at $2,390 in Dec 2025 then delisted; relisted ~17% lower — prior deal likely fell through, $2,390 was over-market

red_flags.how_to_verify: Inspect fire escape/egress on tour; ask about open ECB cases
red_flags.why: Active penalties cite fire-escape landings obstructed by A/C units and equipment, plus a prohibited-locking egress gate ($6,250 May 2026) — life-safety in a walk-up

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask about heat last two winters; check HPD Online; speak with a tenant
red_flags.issue: Recurring heat/hot-water complaints and 'no access' inspection pattern
red_flags.why: Multiple 2024-2026 heat complaints incl. Class C, plus repeated HPD inability to gain access — hints at slow response

red_flags.how_to_verify: Measure on tour; request a floor plan
red_flags.issue: No sqft/floor plan on a 'spacious' unit

red_flags.why: High interest without quick rental can indicate an in-person turnoff (walk-up, condition)

unverified: exact current open HPD count (aggregator 17 open, full paywall)
